title: Set up self-hosted Redis with ioredis
summary: Install ioredis, configure a singleton TCP connection for Node.js long-running servers, and implement caching, pub/sub, and queue patterns with Docker-based Redis.
declaration: I will set up Redis using the ioredis client for a long-running Node.js/Next.js server or self-hosted environment. This uses persistent TCP connections and is suitable for Docker, VPS, and traditional server deployments. If you are in a serverless or edge environment, use @upstash/redis instead (HTTP-based, no persistent connections).
